title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
summary: In Georgian-era England, the spirited Elizabeth Bennet navigates issues of manners, marriage, and class. She initially despises the proud, wealthy Mr. Darcy, but as misunderstandings unravel and his true character is revealed, the two overcome their prejudices and pride to fall in love. Subplots follow her sisters' romances and the family's anxieties over inheritance and reputation.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
summary: An ambitious young scientist, Victor Frankenstein, discovers the secret of animating dead matter and creates a sapient creature from assembled body parts. Horrified by his creation, he abandons it. The rejected, lonely creature seeks companionship and revenge, leading to a tragic pursuit across Europe and the Arctic. Themes include scientific hubris, responsibility, isolation, and what it means to be human.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
summary: Told through letters and journals, an ancient vampire count from Transylvania relocates to England to spread his curse. A small group led by the vampire hunter Van Helsing tracks him, races to save an infected young woman, and ultimately destroys him. Themes of seduction, contagion, faith, and modern science versus old superstition recur throughout.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
summary: A brilliant, eccentric consulting detective in Victorian London solves baffling crimes using sharp observation and deductive reasoning, accompanied by his loyal friend and chronicler, Dr. Watson. Cases range from stolen jewels and disappearances to murder and blackmail, each unraveled through logic and forensic detail.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
summary: After the Trojan War, the hero Odysseus undertakes a perilous ten-year voyage home, facing monsters, vengeful gods, sirens, and temptations. Meanwhile his wife Penelope fends off suitors and his son Telemachus searches for him. A foundational epic of homecoming, cunning, loyalty, and divine intervention.

title: The Time Machine
author: H. G. Wells
summary: A Victorian inventor builds a machine that lets him travel through time. He journeys to the distant future and discovers humanity has split into two species: the gentle, childlike Eloi and the subterranean, predatory Morlocks. The traveler explores a decaying far future before returning to warn of where society's divisions may lead.
